reviews.review_description: No point in running the plasma blade if it uses my jet fuel. Lack of dual weird option is disappointing and the hmg overheats way to fast. Sniper feels fine, though it could use more fire rate. Grenade launcher feels fine good to use. The mech suits feel clunky which is nice given the design. Just add dual weird please and thank you
My son very much enjoys the game but we cannot figure out how to join online matchmaking. Any help?

Laughed the entire time as we shouted for help, got the colonists killed, rezzed teamates only to be killed! The devs added joystick turning so don't be put off by those reviews that downvoted for it being missing. I recommend starting out with difficulty level lower than 1!   Just exit/skip tutorial if you get stuck on jetpack to top of building.
Note to Devs: Please fix tutorial with jetpack to top of building. It is too hard to complete for many people. Even if get to top of building doesn't always register.
Dropteam was a fun time!  It's fun to wander around the open environments and killing bugs.  I haven't had a chance to try the multiplayer yet but it seems like it would be a blast!

I was really waiting for this type of game but the one huge deal breaker for me is that there are no controls for turning! You have to turn in your space to rotate your character. Every fps that I own you can rotate with the right thumb stick. I have a tight space that I game I and already hurt my hand on my desk while playing . Developers Please add the right stick turning!

developer_response.body: Understood! We just pushed an update to add this! I hope you'll give it another try and I'm sorry for the hassle.

reviews.review_description:
Same complaint as a few other reviews. No smooth turn (or even snap turn option)! I can't believe a game would be released like that! Also we need a seating down option, I hate when the game wants to force a gameplay on you.

 Like pavlov there is no seating options and the games just tells you you have to play standing. Not everyone can stand, or stand long. Also like in my case sometime i am tired i want to play seated. It should give me the option.
I know real turning is more immersive but we are not all leaving in a castle with massive empty rooms to play. If i turn i might finish with injuries from punching a wall.

Except for that incredibly huge control fail i think the game looks very nice and is very promising. For now i will keep it and not play, if i see in the 14 days i can return the game no updates are done about the controls I am afraid I will have to return the game

developer_response.body: We just pushed an update to add this! I hope you'll give it another try and I'm sorry for the hassle.

reviews.review_description: This looked like it was a promising game. This goes up to the Developers. I just bought this game in which I refunded it right back just because there was no option in which players could use the remote to do a smooth turnaround instead of using your body. That's too bad because I was going to keep the game and give it a great review that it deserves.

developer_response.body: We just pushed an update to add this! I hope you'll give it another try and I'm sorry for the hassle.

reviews.review_description:
Hello Team,
It would be a great game when you please programm a normal twinstick controll.
Why is it so complicated to do that ? I would like to play such games like a normal shooter without jumping an turning around in my livingroom 😁

developer_response.body: We just pushed an update to add this! I hope you'll give it another try and I'm sorry for the hassle.

reviews.review_title: Great Game and apparently dev react fast
reviews.review_description:
I suppose in some cases not having snap or smooth turning is a hassle. Personally it gives me motion sickness so I always recommend to turn physically..
Anyway this problem seems to have been solved thanks to the reactivity of the devs! 
The game is so fun and very diversified in many ways. Lots of different planets with their own peculiar properties. Different types of missions that make me feel the game is quite unique in VR. Maybe the bestiary of enemies isn't diversified enough for the moment but I imagine that'll grow soon..
Multiplayer is so refreshing with real cooperation skills needed. However the matchmaking system is a bit complex and often buggy. No more sound and crash before the drop off on the planet sometime. Had to restart the headset in many cases. But since the devs are so responding I'm sure this will be solved fast as well.
This is already one of the top games on the Quest and will get bigger and bigger if updates are done properly.

reviews.review_title: I never thought I would like this, I did
reviews.review_description:
The turning issue has been solved but if you're experiencing a weird lag, turn off asynchronous spacewarp in the in-game settings.

This game has a lot of potential and I found the planet zones to be pretty large. I am not into games like Hell Divers but this game has me hooked.

reviews.review_title: Guess being able to turn isn't important
reviews.review_description: Why would you make a game purposely that you can't turn? Left or right?////smh You want everyone to buy a swivel chair? It's 2025 if I have to tell you to implement controls so I can turn left and right which has been a staple in Gaming because of how simplistic it is and been in every single game basically since 1991. Low hopes for this game now. I got to waste one of my refunds on this garbage. I knew I should have bought the other space looking game. At least I can turn left and right this is stupid

developer_response.body:
We hear you loud and clear! We just pushed an update to add this! I hope you'll give it another try and I'm sorry for the hassle.

It was setup to track your actual body facing using Oculus's body tracking, which is great for standing play. But we completely hear you that supporting joystick turning is REALLY important for folks who want to play sitting down, have confined space, etc. So sorry that we didn't include it in the first release!

reviews.review_description: DropTeam delivers an incredible VR experience! The multiplayer gameplay is smooth and intense, with thrilling battles against swarms of alien bugs on stunning, otherworldly landscapes. Whether you're teaming up with friends or diving in solo, every mission is packed with action and excitement. Highly recommended for anyone who loves sci-fi and fast-paced combat!

Doing the missions is exhilarating. Solo player can be a blast. Hooking up a full team and playing a mission can be pretty intense. Once you get everyone working together and handling different assignments in a coordinated effort it is very satisfying. Extremely immersive and a real hoot!

Nothing beats dropping into a spooky alien world with a team of your friends, using the best futuristic weapons and tech you can get, and battling the aliens to accomplish your mission. I love the variety of different planets, monsters, and goals so every game is a new experience. 

And it’s totally immersive in VR. When the bug hordes are after you… grab your grenade launcher and good luck! I love it. ￼
